data.narrative:
Planet Labs is a publicly traded Space & Aerospace company founded by Chris Boshuizen, William Marshall, and Robbie Schingler. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage public comparables: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The median valuation across these three public Space & Aerospace peers is $6.3B, establishing a central reference point. The peer range spans $900M at the low end to $37.0B at the high end, reflecting substantial dispersion in the sector. Public-comp anchoring using price-to-sales multiples (sector median 53.2x across six public tickers including RKLB and PL) cannot be applied because Planet Labs' revenue is not on file, limiting the utility of multiple-based approaches for this analysis.

The triangulated valuation range is $5.4B to $7.2B, centered on $6.3B. This range sits modestly below Planet Labs' stated current valuation of $7B+, suggesting the peer-median approach anchors near but slightly conservative to the current mark. The key uncertainty is sector volatility: the $36.1B spread between the highest and lowest comparable reflects heterogeneity in business models and growth profiles within Space & Aerospace. Additional revenue and growth-rate data would strengthen conviction in any point estimate within this range.
